verdict: 2026-07-16 21:39 PDT (America/Los_Angeles) — The round-4 shape is fundamentally sound. Mounting the narrow deauth router and logger before global JSON parsing does not alter `/api/*`, `/auth/callback`, static assets, or SPA fallback behavior because unmatched requests fall through normally. The route-local `express.raw({ type: () => true })` followed by signature verification and guarded `JSON.parse` is the right idiom for verifying exact bytes before accepting JSON. I would build it this way, with two small middleware-configuration corrections.

findings.title: Blanket validation disablement discards useful limiter safety checks
findings.claim: `validate: false` disables every express-rate-limit diagnostic, not merely the irrelevant IP/proxy checks. That includes checks for invalid store results, double counting, incompatible options, invalid limits, and malformed reset metadata. The constant key generator already bypasses the default IP key generator where the proxy validations run, and this configuration initializes cleanly with validation enabled, so the blanket suppression is unnecessary and can hide a future bad `rateLimitOptions` or store configuration.
findings.alternative: Remove `validate: false` and retain the constant `keyGenerator`. If a specific library warning later proves inapplicable, disable only that named validation through the validation object.
findings.win: Deletes one configuration line while restoring all library-provided configuration and store-integrity checks; no behavior, dependency, or test architecture changes are required.

findings.title: The body-parser error handler silently absorbs unrelated failures
findings.claim: Although documented as handling body-parser failures, this router-wide four-argument middleware catches errors from the limiter, raw parser, and dispatch handler. An unexpected limiter/store or application error without a status is converted to a silent 400, masking an operational failure as a client error and preventing the normal error path from recording it.
findings.alternative: Recognize and terminate only expected raw-body parser errors—such as `entity.too.large` and the other known body-parser request errors—then call `next(err)` for anything else. Keep oversized requests on the bare 413 path.
findings.win: Preserves the quiet bounded 413 response while eliminating a silent failure path and retaining normal diagnostics for genuine limiter or application defects.
